violations.description: Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not properly protected from potential contamination. Container of raw shrimp was found with a used single-use glove on top of the shrimp. Gloves should be properly discarded after use.
Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Dirty towel was found stored on the counter near the slicer. Dirty linens should be placed in the laundry.

General equipment and utensils - Use limitations of certain materials. — --Material of food contact equipment/utensil is not approved for use by food service code. Onions found stored in a black garbage can near the walk-in freezer. Garbage can is not made with a food grade material. Items used for food items should be constructed of food grade materials.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. Filters for the hood ventilation system were found dirty during inspection. These should be cleaned and maintained clean.

Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was not provided in sufficient number and/or not accessible to allow convenient use by employees. Hand washing sink in the dish room has been removed. Hand washing sink should be readily available for washing hands in dish areas.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with waste receptacle as needed. Hand washing sinks throughout the kitchen were found without garbage receptacles. Hand washing sinks should be supplied with waste receptacles.

Employee dressing areas and lockers. — --Employees' clothing/possessions were in an area where contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items may occur. Coats found stored on the shelving units in the dry storage areas. Personal items should be stored separate from food items.

Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Maintenance equipment, supplies and tools were not stored in a manner to protect against contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items. Brooms and dust pans were found stored next to bags of onions. Maintenance items should be stored separate from food items.--Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. Mops found hanging with the mop head above the handle. Mop heads should hang with the head below the handle to prevent contaminating the handle.

Handling and storage of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Secondary container of poisonous or toxic material was not clearly identified to indicated contents. Bottle of blue liquid found on shelf in the dish area without a label. Cleaners and chemical should be properly labeled with contents.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 41F or below during display or service. Refried beans and salsa verde, prepared yesterday, were found in the walk-in cooler at 44-54F. Potentially hazardous food items should be maintained at 41F or below. These items should be discarded.
General employee practices. — --Observed food employee eating food near the drive up window. Food should not be consumed in the kitchen.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. Bare wood found used for shelving and supports in the dry storage area. Bare wood should be sealed so it is nonabsorbent and easily cleanable.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Wire shelving units in the walk-in cooler were found dirty during inspection. These should be cleaned and maintained clean.

Sanitizing solution testing devices. — --Test strip or kit that accurately measures the sanitizing concentration was not provided. Chlorine test strips were the only testing strips available for checking the strength of the lactic acid sanitizing solutions used in the facility. Strips that test lactic acid solution strength should be available for testing the sanitizing solutions used in facility.

Plumbing system. — --Plumbing system was not designed, constructed, operated and/or maintained according to regulation. Hot water was not working at the kitchen hand sink. Hot water must be available for washing hands.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Attachments to walls and ceilings (e.g. light fixtures, vent covers, decorations etc..) were not cleanable and/or maintained in good repair. Ceiling vents and light covers throughout the kitchen were found dirty. These should be cleaned and maintained clean.
Method, temperature and time (5 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
Proper storage, no-reuse (1 pt deducted)
Proper storage, labeling, use (3 pts deducted)
Designed, constructed, installed, and maintained (2 pts deducted)
